name: Google Dorks
purpose: Passive reconnaissance using Google's advanced search operators to surface sensitive data indexed from misconfigured targets
author: Johnny Long (j0hnny)
why_notable: Johnny Long systematized Google Hacking in 2004 with the book 'Google Hacking for Penetration Testers' and the Google Hacking Database (GHDB) at exploit-db.com — now containing 6,500+ community-maintained queries organized by category (exposed credentials, login portals, vulnerable servers, sensitive directories, IoT devices). The technique relies on a fundamental property of search engines: they index everything they can crawl, including content never meant to be public. All queries go to Google's servers, not the target — it's fully passive. Long framed Google as a force multiplier: a security auditor could find exposed admin panels without touching the target because Google already had the index.
